[[Barton-upon-Irwell]], [[Greater Manchester]] | avg_attendance = 4,260 | agg_attendance = 21,297 | high_attendance = 4,796 | low_attendance = 3,350 | top_tries = [[Ken Sio]] (6) | top_goals = [[Tui Lolohea]] (9) | top_points = [[Tui Lolohea]] (30) | prev_season = [[2019 Salford Red Devils season|2019]] | next_season = 2021 | list = yes | list_link = List of Salford Red Devils seasons }} This article details the [[Salford Red Devils]]'s rugby league football club's 2020 season. == Fixtures and results == {{legend2|#DDFFDD|Win|border=1px solid #000000}} {{legend2|#FFFFDD|Draw|border=1px solid #000000}} {{legend2|#FFDDDD|Loss|border=1px solid #000000}} *All fixtures are subject to change === Challenge Cup === {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:85%;" |- !Date and time!!Rnd!!Versus!!H/A!!Venue!!Result!!Score!!Tries!!Goals!!Attendance!!TV!!Report |- style="background:#;" width=20 |   |CANCELLED || 6 ||{{leagueicon|st Helens|12}} [[St Helens RFC|St Helens]] || H || [[AJ Bell Stadium]] || || || || || || || |} Salford Red Devils's sixth round opener was due to be against the [[Super League XXIV|previous season's]] champions [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], to whom they were runners-up in the [[2019 Super League Grand Final|Grand Final]]. However, like all Round 6 ties, it was postponed and eventually cancelled due to the [[COVID-19 pandemic in the United Kingdom|COVID-19 pandemic]].{{citation needed|date=October 2020}} Salford was one of a number of teams to receive a bye to the quarter-finals. {| class="wikitable plainrowheaders" style="text-align:center; width: 60%;" |- !scope="col" style="width:20%"|Round !scope="col"
